artPrompt: A jaded human croupier stands behind a worn felt table, sweeping a fan of cards and scattered coins with one weary hand, a coin frozen mid-flip glinting above the other, his tired smirk lit by sickly neon. Tattered tents and a rigged wheel loom behind. The gesture is practiced, going-through-the-motions ennui. Wide landscape, carnival red and hot gold washes bleeding into smoky black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Two-Bit Croupier
text:
Whenever a player flips one or more coins, you gain 1 life.
{T}: Flip a coin. If you win the flip, add {B}.
flavorText: He's been dealing the same rigged hand for forty years and tips are still bad.
